items.title: Ep12. Why do we need to sleep?
items.desc: In this episode of Little Ears, Big Ideas, Lily and Max explore big question: why do we need to sleep? From brain tidying and memory saving to growing, repairing, dreaming, and avoiding full grumble-monster mode, this cozy bedtime-science episode helps kids understand that sleep is not doing nothing.

items.title: World Cup follow up: What does offside mean?
items.desc: In this World Cup follow-up episode, Lily and Max tackle one of football’s most confusing rules: offside. With a playful referee whistle, a simple pitch example, and plenty of Max-style football drama, they explain why a goal can be cancelled, what the second-last defender means, and why timing matters so much.

items.title: Ep11. Why do humans talk, but dogs bark?
items.desc: In this episode, Lily and Max explore how animals communicate, how human language works, and why dogs use barks, tails, sounds, and body language to share their messages. A fun, woofy episode about words, animals, and big ideas.

items.title: Special Episode. World Cup 2026
items.desc: In this special episode, Lily and Max explore why the World Cup makes the whole world cheer! Get ready for football, facts, feelings, and a celebration of how one game can bring people together around the world.

items.title: Ep10. What Is snot made of?
items.desc: In this episode, Lily and Max explore Arthur’s wonderfully slimy question: what is snot made of? From water and sticky mucins to dust, pollen, and tiny germ-fighting helpers, they discover why our noses make mucus, how it protects our bodies, and why snot sometimes gets runny or changes colour when we’re sick. A funny, slightly gross, and very curious science adventure for little listeners!
